name: Calcium chloride
indications:
## Main indications

Treatment of hypocalcemia and hyperkalemia.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.presentation: Ampoule 10% calcium chloride (13.6 mEq Ca/10 mL).
adulto.dose: Cardiac arrest: 1 g (10 mL) IV/IO. Hypocalcemia: 0.5–1 g slow IV.

pediatrico.dose: Symptomatic hypocalcemia: 0.1 - 0.2 mL/kg/dose (0.14-0.28 mEq Ca++/kg/dose) every 4-6 h. Adults: 5-10 mL/dose (7-14 mEq/dose) every 6 h. Hyperkalemia: 0.3 mL/kg (maximum: 3 mL). See CIME Electrolytes Bulletin.
pediatrico.presentation: 10 mL ampoules: 100 mg of calcium chloride/mL (1.4 mEq Ca++/mL = 27.3 mg Ca++/mL)
pediatrico.notes: IV administration must be performed slowly (< 0.5-1 mL/min. For IV infusion dilute to 20 mg of calcium chloride per mL (0.2 mL/mL) and infuse over 1 hour. Compatible solvents: 5% dextrose and SWFI. Incompatible with bicarbonate, phosphate, and sulfate.

neonatal.dose: 20 mg/kg slow IV in cardiac arrest/hypocalcemia NICU.

stability:
## Stability

- Incompatible with bicarbonate in line.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Tissue necrosis from extravasation. Monitor heart rate. If bradycardia occurs, stop the infusion.
